title: Mammea touriga
intro: Mammea touriga, also known as Touriga, is a plant species in the family Clusiaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Mammea touriga include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mammea touriga is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of various herbal remedies, including infusions and decoctions.
